dialogueChunks.intro: Keep your voice down, {player_name}. The walls in {location} have ears these days, and not all of them are friendly.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The settlement commander has been hoarding supplies while the rest of us scrape by on scraps. {target_npc} caught them diverting {quest_item} from the communal stores to their private stockpile {location_hint}. When confronted, the commander doubled the guard patrols and threatened anyone who speaks up with exile. The people are furious, but they're afraid. We need someone from outside to gather evidence and rally support: someone the commander can't intimidate or banish.
dialogueChunks.outro: We built this settlement together. Every brick, every beam. And now one person acts like they own it all. That ends now.

playerResponses.text: Hoarding while people starve? I'll help you expose the truth.
Political disputes aren't my specialty, but corruption is corruption. I'm in.
Internal power struggles are a hornet's nest. I'd rather not get stung.

dialogueChunks.intro: You picked a hell of a time to visit {location}, {player_name}. Things are about to change around here.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The garrison commander imposed a tax last month: a third of everything we produce goes to the guard barracks. A third! The farmers are breaking their backs and going hungry while the soldiers eat like kings. {target_npc} tried to petition for relief, and the commander had them thrown in the stocks for a day as a warning. That was the last straw. Half the settlement is ready to refuse the tax, but we need {quest_item} to sustain ourselves independently and enough able bodies to hold the line when the commander sends troops to collect.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not rebels, {player_name}. We're people who've been pushed past the breaking point. Will you stand with us?

playerResponses.text: A third of everything? That's not a tax, it's theft. Count me in.
Standing against armed soldiers is risky. What's your plan?
I don't pick sides in local politics. Sort this out yourselves.

dialogueChunks.intro: I'm going to tell you something dangerous, {player_name}. Dangerous for me and dangerous for you, just by hearing it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: There's a merchant consortium that controls all trade in and out of {location}. They set the prices, they decide who buys and who sells, and anyone who tries to trade independently finds their goods confiscated and their stall burned down. {target_npc} had their entire inventory seized last week for undercutting the consortium's prices on {quest_item}. We've been meeting in secret, a group of us who've had enough. We want to establish an independent trading post {location_hint}, outside the consortium's reach, but the route there is infested with {enemy_type} and we need supplies to build it.
dialogueChunks.outro: Freedom to trade shouldn't require permission from people who profit from our misery. Help us build something they can't control.

playerResponses.text: A free trading post? That's a cause worth fighting for.
Breaking a trade monopoly sounds profitable for everyone. I'm listening.
Cross a merchant consortium? Those people have long memories and deep pockets. No thanks.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need to ask you something, and I need you to decide quickly. Before the patrol comes back around.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The warden of {location} has been selling our people's labor to the {enemy_type}. Yes, you heard that right. Sending workers into their territory to mine {quest_item} in exchange for the warden's personal protection. {target_npc} discovered the arrangement when they followed a work crew {location_hint} and saw them handing over ore to {enemy_type} overseers. The workers are terrified: the warden has threatened their families if they talk. We need proof, we need allies, and we need to break this arrangement before more people are forced into it.
dialogueChunks.outro: Our own leader is trading our people's sweat and safety for personal gain. This betrayal cannot stand.

playerResponses.text: Selling labor to hostile creatures? That's slavery with a middleman. I'll help you end it.
This is bigger than a revolt: this is liberation. Tell me the plan.
Going against a warden with {enemy_type} allies? The odds are terrible.
